fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the service document associated with this line.

fields.name: Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer who owns the items to be serviced under the service order.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service order number associated with this line.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the line.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the service line.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of an item, general ledger account, resource code, cost, or standard text.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the inventory location from where the items on the line should be taken and where they should be registered.

fields.name: Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the inventory posting group assigned to the item.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of an item, resource, cost, or a standard text on the line.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional description of the item, resource, or cost.

fields.name: Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the item or resource's unit of measure, such as piece or hour.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of item units, resource hours, cost on the service line.

fields.name: Outstanding Quantity
fields.tooltip:
Defines how many registered items, resources, costs or general ledger account payments on the service lines remain to be shipped.
The program calculates the outstanding quantity by subtracting the value of the Quantity Shipped field from the value of the Quantity field.
The program updates this field automatically when you change the Quantity field or when the shipment of items, resources, costs or G/L account payments is posted.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the items, resources, costs, or general ledger account payments, which should be invoiced.

fields.name: Qty. to Ship
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items that remain to be shipped.

fields.name: Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price of one unit of the item or resource. You can enter a price manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field on the related card.

fields.name: Unit Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost, in LCY, of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: VAT %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT percentage used to calculate Amount Including VAT on this line.

fields.name: Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount defined for a particular group, item, or combination of the two.

fields.name: Line Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount amount that is granted for the item on the line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total net amount on the service line. This amount does not include VAT payment.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of this field by multiplying Unit Price and Quantity, and subtracting Line Discount Amount from the obtained product.
You can not change the contents of the field manually.

fields.name: Amount Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net amount, including VAT, for this line.

fields.name: Allow Invoice Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the invoice line is included when the invoice discount is calculated.

fields.name: Gross Weight
fields.tooltip:
Contains the gross weight of one unit of the item on the line.
The program automatically retrieves the value from the Item table when you fill in the No. field.
The program then uses the value in the Gross Weight field to calculate the total gross weight of all the lines linked to the particular service header.

fields.name: Net Weight
fields.tooltip:
Contains the net weight of one unit of the item on the line.
The program automatically retrieves the net weight from the Item table when you fill in the No. field.
The program uses the value in the Net Weight field to calculate the total net weight of all the lines linked to the particular service header.

fields.name: Units per Parcel
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many units one parcel of the item on the line contains.
The program retrieves the quantity from the Item table when you fill in the No. field.
The program uses the value in the Units per Parcel field to determine the total quantity of item units on all the lines linked to the particular service header.

fields.name: Unit Volume
fields.tooltip:
Contains the volume of one unit of the item on the line.
The program automatically retrieves the volume from the Item table when you fill in the No. field.
The program uses the value in the Unit Volume field to calculate the total volume of items on all the lines linked to the particular service header.

fields.name: Appl.-to Item Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the document or journal line is applied to.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Customer Price Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of the customer price group.
The program automatically copies the price group code from the Customer Price Group field in the Service header.
Based on the customer price group, the program determines whether the customer should pay a price different from the one specified in the Unit Price field on the Item card. Price conditions are described in the Sales Prices window for the particular customer price group. You can view the Sales Prices window by clicking Cust. Price Group, Sales Prices in the Customer Price Groups window.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related job.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related job task.

fields.name: Job Line Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of journal line that is created in the Job Planning Line table from this line.

fields.name: Work Type Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the type of work performed by the resource registered on this line.

fields.name: Outstanding Amount
fields.tooltip:
Contains the amount that the customer should pay for the items, resource hours, or costs that still need to be shipped. The program automatically updates this field when you change the quantity or ship the items, resource hours, costs or G/L account payment on the service line.
The program calculates the amount as Amount Including VAT multiplied by Outstanding Quantity and divided by Quantity.

fields.name: Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Indicates how many units of the item, resource hours, cost, or general ledger account payments on the service line have been shipped but not yet invoiced or consumed.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the value in the field by extracting the Quantity Invoiced and Quantity Consumed fields values from the Quantity Shipped field value.

You cannot change the contents of this field. You cannot delete a line containing a quantity of items, costs, resource hours or G/L account payments that have been shipped but not invoiced or consumed.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the amount on the service line that has been shipped to the customer but neither invoiced nor consumed yet.
The program updates the field when you post. The value in this field is calculated by multiplying Amount Including VAT by Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced and then dividing by Quantity.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Quantity Shipped
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as shipped.

fields.name: Quantity Invoiced
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as invoiced.

fields.name: Shipment No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the correspondent shipment in the posted shipment list.

fields.name: Shipment Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the number the current service line has in the posted shipment list.
The program automatically copies the number from the posted shipment list as soon as you have posted the line.
You cannot change the contents in this field.

fields.name: Bill-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer that you send or sent the invoice or credit memo to.

fields.name: Inv. Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total calculated invoice discount amount for the line.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Calculation Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies which VAT calculation type is used when this service line is posted.
The program copies the value from the VAT Calculation Type field in the VAT Posting Setup window. The program retrieves the VAT calculation type that corresponds to the VAT Bus. Posting Group and VAT Prod. Posting Group fields on the service line.
The VAT calculation type determines how the VAT is calculated when this line is posted. There are three different VAT types: Normal VAT, Reverse Charge VAT, and Full VAT, and there is also one type for use with sales tax.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the transaction type.
For each new line, the program automatically retrieves the transaction type code from the Transaction Type field in the Service Header table. The field is blank if no code was entered on the service header.
You can manually change the contents of the field on individual service lines.
This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip:
Contains the transport method code. The program automatically retrieves the code from the Transport Method field on the service header of the document. The field is blank if no transport method has been entered on the header.
You can change the contents of the field on the service lines.

fields.name: Attached to Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the service line to which the line containing the extended text is attached.
When you run the Insert Ext. Text function, Microsoft Dynamics NAV copies the Line No. field from the service line that contains the item, resource, cost, or standard text that is described by the extended text.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Exit Point
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of the exit port through which you ship the service out of your country/region. The program retrieves the code automatically from the Exit Point field on the service header of the document.
You can not change the contents of the field on the service lines.
This information is used for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the area of the customer's address.
For each new line, the program automatically retrieves the code from the Area field on the header of the service document. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual service lines. This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the type of transaction specification used for the item on the current line.
For each new line, the program automatically retrieves the code from the Transaction Specification field in the Service Header table. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual service lines.
This information is necessary for INSTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies that items, resources, or costs on the current credit memo line are liable for sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sales tax group code to which this item belongs.

fields.name: VAT Clause Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT Clause Code that is associated with the sales line.
After you post a sales document, the VAT Clause Code that is associated with the document is copied to the service line.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved item or resource to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code for the amounts on this line.

fields.name: Outstanding Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the amount (in LCY) that the customer should pay to the general ledger account, for the items, resource hours, or costs that still need to be shipped before the order is completed.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the contents of this field by using the value in the Outstanding Amount field and the relevant exchange rate.
If the Currency Code field on the service line is blank, outstanding amount (LCY) equals to the value in the Outstanding Amount field.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the amount on the service line that has been shipped to the customer but not yet invoiced or consumed. The amount is expressed in LCY.
The program updates the field when you post. The value in this field is calculated by multiplying Amount Including VAT by Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced and dividing by Quantity. The value is then converted to LCY using the relevant exchange rate.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many item units on this line have been reserved.

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether a reservation can be made for items on this line.

fields.name: VAT Base Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that serves as a base for calculating the Amount Including VAT field.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip:
Contains the cost per unit of an item, resource or cost on the service line. The unit for which the cost is displayed is specified in the Unit of Measure field on the line.
The program calculates the unit cost using the value in the Unit Cost (LCY) field and the exchange rate of the currency specified in the Currency Code field on the service line. If the Currency Code field is blank, the unit cost is expressed in LCY, and therefore, is equal to the value in the Unit Cost (LCY) field.

fields.name: System-Created Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the entry is related to a service charge levied on the specific customer and has been automatically created by the program. Conditions for service charges on invoices for different customers are set up in the Cust. Invoice Discounts window. The program uses the value in the Service Charge field in the Cust. Invoice Discounts window to create a corresponding line when you run the Calculate Invoice Discount function.

fields.name: Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value of products on the worksheet line.

fields.name: VAT Difference
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the difference between the calculated VAT amount and the VAT amount that you have entered manually, if you choose to manually alter a VAT amount in the VAT Amount field.
The VAT difference displayed here is distributed proportionally between service lines with the same VAT identifier.

fields.name: Inv. Disc. Amount to Invoice
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the discount amount calculated for the items, resource hours, general ledger account payments, or costs that should be invoiced as indicated in the Qty. to Invoice field.
The program calculates the discount only if there is a check mark in the Allow Invoice Disc. field on the service line. The invoice discount amount depends on the value selected in the Discount % field in the Cust. Invoice Discounts window. You cannot change the value in the Inv. Disc. Amount to Invoice field manually.

fields.name: VAT Identifier
fields.tooltip:
Contains an identifier used to group VAT Posting Setups with similar attributes.
The program copies the VAT identifier from the VAT Identifier field in the VAT Posting Setup table. The program retrieves the relevant VAT identifier based on the combination of the values in the VAT Bus. Posting Group field and the VAT Prod. Posting Group field for this particular service line.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Time Sheet No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a time sheet. A number is assigned to each time sheet when it is created. You cannot edit the number.

fields.name: Time Sheet Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a time sheet line. A number is assigned to each line when it is created. You cannot edit the number.

fields.name: Time Sheet Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the time sheet was created.

fields.name: Job Planning Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project planning line number associated with this line. This establishes a link that can be used to calculate actual usage.

fields.name: Job Remaining Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity that remains to complete a project.

fields.name: Job Remaining Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity that remains to complete a job, expressed in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Job Remaining Total Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the remaining total cost, as the sum of costs from project planning lines associated with the order.

fields.name: Job Remaining Total Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the remaining total cost for the project planning line associated with the service order.

fields.name: Job Remaining Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net amount of the project planning line.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where the items are picked or put away.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip:
Contains the quantity of items, resource hours, or costs per unit of measure specified in the Unit of Measure field on the service line.
The program automatically fills in this field when you enter the item number, resource code, or cost code on the service line.

fields.name: Planned
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the service line is included in production calculations.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items, resource time, costs, or general ledger account payments, expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Outstanding Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of registered items, resource time, costs, or payments to the general ledger account that have not been shipped.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many of the registered items, resources, costs, or general ledger account payments are still scheduled for invoicing. This quantity is expressed in the base units of measure. The program updates this field automatically when the Qty. to Invoice field on the service line changes.
The program calculates the value in this field by multiplying the value in the Qty. to Invoice field by the value in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

fields.name: Qty. to Ship (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity of items, expressed in the based unit of measure, that will be posted as shipped from the service order line.
For more information, see Qty. to Ship.

fields.name: Qty. Shipped Not Invd. (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Indicates how many units of the item, resource hours, cost, or general ledger account payments on the service line have been shipped but not yet invoiced or consumed. The quantity is expressed in base units of measure specified in the Unit of Measure field.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the value of the field by extracting the Qty. Invoiced (Base) and Qty. Consumed (Base) fields values from the Qty. Shipped (Base) field value.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Qty. Shipped (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the quantity of registered items, resource hours, general ledger account payments, or costs already shipped to the customer. The quantity in the field is expressed in the base units of measure. The program updates this field when the quantity shipped value on the service line is changed.
To calculate the value in this field, the program multiplies the value of the Quantity Shipped field by the value in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

fields.name: Qty. Invoiced (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the quantity of registered items, resource hours, payments to general ledger account, or costs, already invoiced. The quantity in the field is expressed in the base units of measure. The program updates this field when the quantity invoiced on the service line is changed.
To calculate the value in this field, the program multiplies the value of the Quantity Invoiced field by the value in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of registered items that have been reserved.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Contains the name of the responsibility center for the item on the service line. The program copies the information from the Responsibility Center field in the Service Header table. If you change the responsibility center in the service header, you should update information on the corresponding service lines manually.

fields.name: Substitution Available
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether a substitute is available for the item.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of the item category that the item on the line belongs to. The program fills in this field if the type of the service line is Item.
The program copies the item category code from the Item Category Code field in the Item Card window when you fill in the No. field on the service line.
Item categories are set up in the Item Category window, and include default values for posting groups, costing methods, and tax groups. Item categories are used when new items are created.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the item is a catalog item.

fields.name: Item Reference No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the referenced item number. If you enter a cross reference between yours and your vendor's or customer's item number, then this number will override the standard item number when you enter the reference number on a service document.

fields.name: Whse. Outstanding Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units on the service line remain to be handled in the warehouse. The units are expressed in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Completely Shipped
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that the current service line has been shipped completely.
The program inserts a check mark as soon as the value in the Outstanding Quantity field on the line becomes equal to zero.
You cannot change the contents of the field manually.

fields.name: Requested Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the item was requested for delivery in the service order.

fields.name: Promised Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that you promised to deliver the item in the service order.

fields.name: Shipping Time
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the time that is required from when items on service lines are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered to the customer's address.
The value is copied from the Shipping Time field on the service order, which in turn is copied from the customer card.

fields.name: Planned Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the planned date that the shipment will be delivered at the customer's address. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates whether the items will be available for delivery on this date. If the items are available, the planned delivery date will be the same as the requested delivery date. If not, the program calculates the date that the items are available for delivery and enters this date in the Planned Delivery Date field.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code that represents a shipping agent.
The code is copied from the Shipping Agent Service Code field on the service order when you enter an item on the service line. You can change the code if necessary.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Service Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code that represents a shipping agent service.
This field is copied from the Shipping Agent Service Code field on the service order, which in turn is copied from the Shipping Agent Service Code field on the customer card, or it is entered manually.
If you enter a code in the Planned Delivery Date field, then the Shipping Time field in theShipping Agent Services table is copied to the Shipping Time field on the service line.

fields.name: Appl.-from Item Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the document or journal line is applied from.

fields.name: Service Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service item number linked to this service line.

fields.name: Appl.-to Service Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service ledger entry number this line is applied to.

fields.name: Service Item Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service item line number linked to this service line.

fields.name: Service Item Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the serial number of the service item to which this service line is linked.

fields.name: Service Item Line Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the service item line in the service order.

fields.name: Serv. Price Adjmt. Gr. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the service price adjustment group code that applies to this line.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the service line should be posted.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip:
Contains the date when the order was created.
The program fills in this field automatically. You can not change the order date manually.

fields.name: Needed by Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when you require the item to be available for a service order.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for an alternate shipment address if you want to ship to another address than the one that has been entered automatically. This field is also used in case of drop shipment.

fields.name: Qty. to Consume
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items, resource hours, costs, or G/L account payments that should be consumed.

fields.name: Quantity Consumed
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items, resource hours, costs, or general ledger account payments on this line, which have been posted as consumed.

fields.name: Qty. to Consume (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the quantity of the registered items, resources, costs or general ledger account payments that should be posted as consumed. The quantity is expressed in the base units of measure. The program updates this field automatically when the Qty. to Consume field on the service line changes.
The program calculates the value in this field by multiplying the value in the Qty. to Consume field by the value in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

fields.name: Qty. Consumed (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of units of items, resource hours, costs, or general ledger account payments registered on the line that have already been posted as consumed. The quantity is expressed in the base units of measure. The program updates this field when the quantity consumed value on the service line is changed.
To calculate the value in this field, the program multiplies the value of the Quantity Consumed field by the value in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.
You cannot change the value in the field manually.

fields.name: Service Price Group Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of the Service Price Group of the service item that corresponds to the current service line.
For each new line, the program automatically copies the code from the Service Price Group Code field on the corresponding service item line.

fields.name: Fault Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the fault area associated with this line.

fields.name: Symptom Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the symptom associated with this line.

fields.name: Fault Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the fault associated with this line.

fields.name: Resolution Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the resolution associated with this line.

fields.name: Exclude Warranty
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the warranty discount is excluded on this line.

fields.name: Warranty
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a warranty discount is available on this line of type Item or Resource.

fields.name: Contract No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the contract, if the service order originated from a service contract.

fields.name: Contract Disc. %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the contract discount percentage that is valid for the items, resources, and costs on this line.

fields.name: Warranty Disc. %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the warranty discount that is valid for the items or resources on this line.

fields.name: Component Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the number of the line in the Service Item Component List window. This line contains the service item component that has been replaced by the item on the service line.
When you select the replaced component in the Service Item Component List window, the program copies the value of the Line No. field from the Service Item Component List window to the Component Line No. field on the service line.

fields.name: Spare Part Action
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the item was used to replace the whole service item, one of the service item components, installed as a new component, or used as a supplementary tool.

fields.name: Fault Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the fault reason for this service line.

fields.name: Replaced Item No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of the service item component that has been replaced by the item on the service line.
The program copies the number from the No. field when you select replaced component in the Service Item Component List window.

fields.name: Exclude Contract Discount
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the contract discount is excluded for the item, resource, or cost on this line.

fields.name: Replaced Item Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the service item component that has been replaced by the item on the service line. The options are Service Item or Item.

fields.name: Price Adjmt. Status
fields.tooltip:
Indicates whether the price on the service line was adjusted automatically or manually. The options are: Adjusted or Modified. Adjusted indicates that the service price adjustment on the line was performed automatically. Modified means that you changed the service price adjustment amounts manually.
The program fills in the field after you run the Adjust Service Price function.
You can not manually change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Line Discount Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the line discount assigned to this line.

fields.name: Copy Components From
fields.tooltip:
Specifies an option indicating where the components should be copied from to the component list when you replace a service item.
You can select among the following options:


Field


Description


Item BOM

Components are copied from the Item BOM.


Old Service Item

Components are copied from the old service item.


Old Serv.Item w/o Serial No.

Components are copied from the old service item, but the serial number is not copied.


None

The component list for the new service item is not created.

fields.name: Return Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code explaining why the item was returned.

fields.name: Allow Line Disc.
fields.tooltip:
Indicates whether a discount on the service costs should be calculated.
The program retrieves this value from the Allow Line Disc. field on the service header. If this field contains a checkmark, the line discount will be calculated for the current service line.
You can remove a check mark from the field, which will mean that no discount will be applicable to the current line.

fields.name: Customer Disc. Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the customer discount group of the bill-to customer.
The program copies the code automatically from the Customer Disc. Group field on the service header of the document.
You can also change the contents of this field manually.

fields.name: Qty. Picked
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units of the item that have been registered as picked with regard to the service order.

fields.name: Qty. Picked (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many items have already been moved or picked for the service item line. The units are expressed in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Completely Picked
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the service line items have been completely picked from the warehouse.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Pick Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the service order are currently on warehouse pick lines. The units are expressed in the base unit of measure.
